name: Potential UAC Bypass
notes: This rule triggers when behaviour associated with bypassing Windows User Account Control is detected.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (Non-System Directory)
BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (System Directory)
BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Elevated COM Object
BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - IE Add-On Installer
BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Mock System Directory (Image)
BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Mock System Directory (Process)
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (Non-System Directory), BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (System Directory), BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Elevated COM Object, BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - IE Add-On Installer, BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Mock System Directory (Image), BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Mock System Directory (Process), BB:CategoryDefinition: UAC Bypass Registry Key
TD_list: APPLY Potential UAC Bypass on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (Non-System Directory), BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (System Directory), BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Elevated COM Object, BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - IE Add-On Installer, BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Mock System Directory (Image), BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - Mock System Directory (Process), BB:CategoryDefinition: UAC Bypass Registry Key
RED_list:
Event description: Detects when a behaviour that may indicate attempts to bypass Windows User Account Control enforcement occurs. This can be as a result of DLL Hijacking or Registry modification. 
This can indicate a silent privilege escalation.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
